Ferramentas
Origem: Wikipédia, a enciclopédia livre.
Vasco Lourenço da Cunha
Nascimento	1210
Cidadania	Reino de Portugal
Progenitores	
Lourenço Fernandes da Cunha
Filho(a)(s)	Martim Vasques da Cunha
Irmão(ã)(s)	Gomes Lourenço da Cunha, Martim Lourenço da Cunha, Urraca Lourenço da Cunha
Ocupação	terratenente
[edite no Wikidata]
Vasco Lourenço da Cunha (1210 -?) foi um nobre do Reino de Portugal e o 2.º senhor do morgado de Tábua[1][2].

Relações familiares
Foi filho de Lourenço Fernandes da Cunha (1180 -?) e de Sancha Lourenço Macieira filha de Lourenço Gomes de Maceira. Casou com D. Teresa Pires Portel (c. 1210 – 1291) filha de D. Pedro Pires de Portel ou D. Pedro Pires de Portel ou ainda D. Pedro Pires de Portugal e de D. Froile Rodrigues de Pereira, de quem teve:

Estevão Vasques da Cunha, 3.º senhor do Morgado de Tábua;
Martim Vasques da Cunha (1235 -?)[3]., 4.º senhor do Morgado de Tábua casado com Joana Rodrigues de Nomães;
Inês Vasques da Cunha (1240 -?) casou com Afonso Mendes de Melo, 2.º senhor de Melo;
Sancha Vasques da Cunha casou com Fernando Gonçalves Coronel;
Teresa Vasques da Cunha, que foi freira no Mosteiro de Arouca.
Ver também

nameMeanings.name: Lourenço
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Francisco, Fernando, Abilio, Mario, Albino, Armando, Luis, Luiz, Adalberto.  Portuguese Joaquim, Joao, Adauto, Anabela, Duarte, Goncalo, Leao, Paulo, Valentim.
Portuguese (Lourenço): from the personal name Lourenço, Latin Laurentius (see Lawrence ).

variants.name: Lawrence
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Lorens, Laurence, from Latin Laurentius ‘man from Laurentum’, a place in Italy probably named from its laurels or bay trees. The name was borne by a Christian saint who was martyred at Rome in the 3rd century AD ; he enjoyed a considerable cult throughout Europe, with consequent popularity of the personal name (French Laurent, Italian, Spanish Lorenzo, Catalan Llorenç, Portuguese Lourenço, German Laurenz, Polish Wawrzyniec, etc.). In Britain this is a common name from the 12th century, with pet forms such as Law , Low , Lawrie , Laurie , Larry , Larkin , all of which are represented in surnames. There was also a feminine form Laurencia which may have given rise to the English surname. The surname is also borne by Jews among whom it is presumably an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Ashkenazic surnames. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Lorenz , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Slovenian Lavrenčič and Lovrenčič (patronymics from Lavrencij and Lovrenc, equivalents of Lawrence), Polish Wawrzyniak . Compare Larrance , Laurence , Lawerence , Lieurance , and Lowrance .

nameMeanings.name: da
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mandarin form of the surname 達, said to be derived from the personal name Shu Da (叔達), a talent during the reign of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437  BC ); or adopted as a Han surname by other minority ethnic groups.
Mandarin form of the surname 笪, a surname originating in ancient Jianzhou (located in present-day Fujian province) and Jurong (located in Jiangsu province) or a surname from the Hui ethnic group.

nameMeanings.name: Cunha,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Mario, Luiz, Fernando, Ricardo, Domingos, Eduardo, Jorge, Alberto, Americo.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo, Agostinho, Albano, Conceicao, Henrique, Margarida, Vasco, Zulmira.
Portuguese: habitational name from any of numerous places called Cunha; the ancient form of the placename is Cuinha, probably meaning ‘kitchen’ (from Latin culina). Compare Da Cunha .

variants.name: Cuna
meanings: Italian (mainly Apulia): perhaps a nickname from cuna ‘crib’.
Portuguese: nickname related to cuna ‘crib’, or perhaps a variant of Cunha .

variants.name: Da Cunha
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Afonso, Albano, Almir, Ilidio, Serafim.  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Mario, Carlos, Alvaro, Fernando, Jorge, Rogerio, Alfonso, Alfredo, Armando, Armindo.
Portuguese: variant of Cunha , with the addition of da, a shortened form of the preposition de ‘from’ and the feminine definite article a ‘the’.

nameMeanings.name: Vasco
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of the medieval Spanish personal name Velasco or Belasco, from which is derived the surname Velásquez. The modern name has the same form as the Spanish adjective vasco ‘Basque’.

en.lifeSummary: When Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua was born in 1195, in Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portugal, his father, Lourenço Fernandes da Cunha, was 50 and his mother, Maria Sancha Lourenço de Maceira, was 15. He had at least 3 sons and 4 daughters with Teresa Pires Portel. He died in 1262, in Portugal, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua est né en 1195, à Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portugal, son père, Lourenço Fernandes da Cunha, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Maria Sancha Lourenço de Maceira, avait 15 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles avec Teresa Pires Portel. Il est décédé en 1262, à Portugal, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua 1195 in Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portugal geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lourenço Fernandes da Cunha 50 und seine Mutter Maria Sancha Lourenço de Maceira 15. Er hatte mit Teresa Pires Portel mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb 1262 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Portugal.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua è nato il nel 1195 nel Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portogallo, il suo padre, Lourenço Fernandes da Cunha, aveva 50 anni e il suo madre, Maria Sancha Lourenço de Maceira, aveva 15 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie con Teresa Pires Portel. Morì nel 1262, nel Portogallo, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábuaが1195年にポルトガルブラガブラガクウンハで生まれた時，父Lourenço Fernandes da Cunhaは50歳，母Maria Sancha Lourenço de Maceiraは15歳であった。彼はTeresa Pires Portelとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1262年にポルトガルで67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua(이)가 1195년 포르투갈 브라가 브라가 쿤하에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lourenço Fernandes da Cunha(은)는 50세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria Sancha Lourenço de Maceira(은)는 15세였다. 그는 Teresa Pires Portel(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1262년 포르투갈에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua nasceu em 1195, em Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portugal, seu pai, Lourenço Fernandes da Cunha, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Maria Sancha Lourenço de Maceira, tinha 15 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas com Teresa Pires Portel. Ele faleceu em 1262, em Portugal, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua родился в 1195 г. в местности куна, брага, брага, Португалия, его отцу по имени Lourenço Fernandes da Cunha было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria Sancha Lourenço de Maceira, 15. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери от Teresa Pires Portel. Он умер в 1262 г. в местности Португалия в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Vasco Lourenço da Cunha, 2nd Lord of the Morgado de Tábua nació en 1195, en Cunha, Braga, Braga, Portugal, su papá, Lourenço Fernandes da Cunha, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Maria Sancha Lourenço de Maceira, tenía 15 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas con Teresa Pires Portel. Murió en 1262, en Portugal, a la edad de 67 años.
